# ICML 2024

**Coverage status:** Pending

**Checked on:** 2026-07-11

**Official audit sources:** [Source 1](<https://proceedings.mlr.press/v235/>) · [Source 2](<https://proceedings.mlr.press/v235/assets/bib/bibliography.bib>) · [Source 3](<https://icml.cc/virtual/2024/papers.html>) · [Source 4](<https://icml.cc/virtual/2024/events/workshop>) · [Source 5](<https://icml.cc/virtual/2024/affinity_events>) · [Source 6](<https://icml.cc/virtual/2024/workshop/29970>) · [Source 7](<https://icml.cc/virtual/2024/workshop/29955>) · [Source 8](<https://openreview.net/group?id=ICML.cc/2024/Workshop/ML4LMS>) · [Source 9](<https://icml.cc/virtual/2024/workshop/29963>) · [Source 10](<https://openreview.net/group?id=ICML.cc%2F2024%2FWorkshop%2FLLMs_and_Cognition>) · [Source 11](<https://icml.cc/virtual/2024/session/35291>)

**Coverage notes:** All 2,610 PMLR records and all 75 position papers were screened. The side-program audit accounted for all 34 official units: 30 workshops, three affinity-roster events, and the Affinity Joint Poster Session. Twenty-seven workshops have complete paper-level screens, and two eligible workshop papers were already cataloged. Workshop and affinity remain omitted from tracks\_checked because four exact roster blockers remain: Long-Context Foundation Models (29970) lacks its physical and virtual poster titles; ML for Life and Material Science (29955) lacks both poster rosters and its linked OpenReview venue is challenged; Large Language Models and Cognition (29963) lacks complete contributed-talk and poster rosters and its OpenReview venue is challenged; and the Affinity Joint Poster Session (35291) is an empty official shell with no poster titles, authors, event mapping, or paper links.

3 verified papers curated for direct relevance to quantitative finance and asset management. Tracks are separated so main-conference, position, and workshop status remains visible.

[← Back to the main index](../../README.md)

## Main Conference (1)

### [Autonomous Sparse Mean-CVaR Portfolio Optimization](<https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mlresearch/v235/main/assets/lin24w/lin24w.pdf>)

**Catalog ID:** 2024-icml-lin-autonomous-sparse-mean-cvar

**Authors:** Yizun Lin, Yangyu Zhang, Zhao-Rong Lai, Cheng Li

**Venue / year:** ICML · 2024

**Track / presentation:** Main · Poster

**Status / verified:** Published · 2026-07-11

**Topics:** Portfolio Optimization · Risk Management

**Assets / frequency:** Not specified

**Focus:** Builds a sparse mean-CVaR portfolio model that adapts its asset-selection mechanism as the investable universe changes.

**Why it matters:** Couples downside-risk control with scalable security selection instead of requiring a fixed asset pool.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://proceedings.mlr.press/v235/lin24w.html>) · [Paper](<https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mlresearch/v235/main/assets/lin24w/lin24w.pdf>)

---

## Workshops (2)

### [Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Derivative-Constrained PDEs](<https://openreview.net/forum?id=9pFHmyx4Sh>)

**Catalog ID:** 2024-icml-hoshisashi-derivative-constrained-pdes

**Authors:** Kentaro Hoshisashi, Carolyn Phelan, Paolo Barucca

**Venue / year:** ICML · 2024

**Track / presentation:** Workshop · Poster

**Subvenue:** AI for Science: Scaling in AI for Scientific Discovery

**Status / verified:** Accepted · 2026-07-11

**Topics:** Derivatives · Financial Forecasting

**Assets / frequency:** Not specified

**Identifiers:** OpenReview 9pFHmyx4Sh

**Focus:** Extends physics-informed networks to PDEs with derivative constraints and benchmarks the method on option pricing and local-volatility calibration.

**Why it matters:** Enforces economically relevant derivative conditions while learning pricing and volatility surfaces from sparse observations.

**Notes:** ICML 2024 AI for Science workshop paper; acceptance and poster format are shown on the official ICML record.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://icml.cc/virtual/2024/36827>) · [Paper](<https://openreview.net/forum?id=9pFHmyx4Sh>)

---

### [You Shall Pass: Dealing with the Zero-Gradient Problem in Predict and Optimize for Convex Optimization](<https://openreview.net/forum?id=vmnfrrLATL>)

**Catalog ID:** 2024-icml-veviurko-you-shall-pass

**Authors:** Grigorii Veviurko, Wendelin Boehmer, Mathijs de Weerdt

**Venue / year:** ICML · 2024

**Track / presentation:** Workshop · Poster

**Subvenue:** Differentiable Almost Everything Workshop

**Status / verified:** Accepted · 2026-07-11

**Topics:** Portfolio Optimization

**Assets / frequency:** Not specified

**Identifiers:** OpenReview vmnfrrLATL

**Focus:** Repairs zero gradients in decision-focused learning and tests the resulting surrogate on a convex portfolio-optimization problem.

**Why it matters:** Makes it possible to train prediction models against downstream portfolio quality when the optimizer otherwise supplies no learning signal.

**Notes:** ICML 2024 Differentiable Almost Everything Workshop paper; acceptance and poster format are shown on the official ICML record.

**Links:** [Official venue record](<https://icml.cc/virtual/2024/37251>) · [Paper](<https://openreview.net/forum?id=vmnfrrLATL>)
